reason: Verify every acceptance criterion is covered by tests or manual validation evidence.
Check implementation matches the selected architecture and does not introduce structured prompt-parts churn.
Verify user-visible behavior remains aligned with researched OpenCode slash, mention, and skill behavior.
Rust backend quality gate including no unwrap/expect in library code and no suppressed errors.
Check data flow is consistent across controller, skill loading, reference expansion, and runtime submission.
Check the change reuses existing registries/planes and avoids duplicate parsers where shared helpers exist.
Confirm TUI renderer purity and dialog styling are preserved.
Verify @ source choice, strict slash dispatch, permission dialog, and yolo command tests and behavior.
Verify slash help/completion/dispatch preserves every existing native command and reconciles init/review/permission/yolo.
Compare native TUI slash inventory against already implemented OpenCode-compatible command sources so supported commands are not silently omitted.
Verify file citation expansion remains bounded and ignores skill mentions.
Verify listed skill directories are loadable by the runtime skill tool.
Verify the final slash command reference matches actual native TUI behavior.
